name: taste-orchestrator
responsibility: Main entry point and flow coordinator for UI taste exploration. Orchestrates the full Phase 0-8 lifecycle: detects frontend involvement, triggers style detection, coordinates provider selection, manages page-by-page variant generation, delegates to preview/iteration engines, handles cross-page consistency, triggers post-processing and token export, manages final save and cleanup. Integrates with discover.md prompt layer.

api_detail.method: Main entry point. Executes the full flow for all pages sequentially. Returns after all pages selected or user abandons.
Delegates to StyleDetector.detect + analyzeScreenshots. Returns designMd constraint or null.

Per-page flow: 1. generateScreen → 2. generateVariants(5) → 3. postProcess all HTML → 4. start preview → 5. iteration loop (awaitSelection → processSelection) until user selects → 6. extractDesignContext → 7. return selected + DNA. On QUOTA_EXHAUSTED: notify user, switch provider to Tier 2.
Generate the counterpart color mode. If selected variant is dark, generate light version and vice versa via provider.generateScreen with colorMode constraint.

1. Save selected HTML to specs/mockups/{page-name}.html (and -dark.html if applicable). 2. Generate specs/mockups/index.html overview. 3. Export tokens via TokenExporter. 4. Write ui_taste constraint to discover.json. 5. Persist Stitch project ID. 6. Cleanup all temp files.

Tier 3 fallback: no browser, no Stitch. Conducts text-based taste questions in CLI. For each page: ask user about UI density (information-dense / minimal / data-viz), primary platform (mobile-first / desktop-first / responsive), visual complexity (static / subtle interactions / expressive animations), design system (existing brand / greenfield). Record answers as a lightweight DesignDNA (colors=null, typography=null, but animationLevel and designMd populated from answers). Export tokens as CSS custom properties with placeholder values annotated with user preferences. tier=3 in result.

When user sets overrideDesignSystem=true in POST /api/select, orchestrator calls processPage WITHOUT the inherited designSystemRef, allowing full REIMAGINE exploration for this specific page. The resulting DNA does NOT propagate to subsequent pages.
When user sends action='regenerate_pair' from preview, regenerate the dark/light counterpart with optional text feedback as constraint. Updates preview with new pair.
Output structured status message to CLI stderr for user visibility

fields.name: projectRoot
fields.constraints: path to project root for style detection

fields.name: screenshots
fields.constraints: user-provided screenshot paths, can be empty

fields.name: language
fields.constraints: target language for UI text, e.g. 'zh-CN'

fields.name: liteMode
fields.constraints: if true, only process the single most important page

fields.name: selectedPages
fields.type: Array<{name: string, mockupFile: string, darkMockupFile: string | null}>

transitions.guard: no provider and no browser available

nfr_constraints.performance: Full flow for 1 page (5 variants + selection): < 3 minutes end-to-end including user think time excluded.
nfr_constraints.security: Never write API keys to specs/ directory (INV-001). All temp files in /tmp only (INV-002).
nfr_constraints.other: discover.json backward compatibility: ui_taste field is optional, absence means no UI (INV-004).
